page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-80 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 80 vCPUs mapped to 40 physical cores with multithreading enabled, providing dedicated CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 640 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 108034.75 and a PassMark CPU score of 69643.29. Memory performance is highly capable for cached operations, achieving a small-block read-only bandwidth of 4733.91 GB/sec. However, uncached large-block memory operations are average, with read-only bandwidth at 123.55 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 63.07 GB/sec. The instance excels in database operations, scoring 25094.59 on PassMark database tests and handling 67.8 million Redis SET operations per second.

While the instance demonstrates strong capabilities in software compilation, ray tracing, and small-model LLM inference, it exhibits clear limitations for larger AI workloads. Text generation speed for a large 70B LLM model drops to a poor 2.4 tokens per second due to the lack of hardware acceleration. Given its high resource density and memory-to-vCPU ratio, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound enterprise workloads. It is best suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching layers, software build pipelines, and moderate data processing tasks that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-80 is a memory-optimized server configured with 80 vCPUs and 640 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor on the x86_64 architecture. It features 40 physical cores with dedicated CPU allocation and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show strong multi-core performance, with a PassMark CPU score of 69643.29 and exceptional Redis SET throughput of 67.8 million operations per second. While cached memory bandwidth is strong at 4733.91 GB/sec, uncached large-block memory performance and large-model LLM text generation are limited. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive enterprise applications, making it highly suitable for large databases, in-memory caching, and software compilation pipelines.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-80 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 80 vCPUs, 640 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC 7B13 processors, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-80 features 80 vCPUs, 640 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC processors, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized platform featuring eighty vCPUs and massive RAM capacity for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 80 vCPUs and 640 GB RAM powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processors
Strong multi-core performance with dedicated CPU allocation
Excellent throughput for database operations and Redis caching
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for large databases, in-memory caching, and build pipelines
